---
applyTo: "**"
---
# CLEAR — AI Coding Assistant Instructions

This repository uses the **CLEAR** framework for AI-assisted development. Read `clear/principles.md` and `clear/autonomy.yml` at the start of every session.

## Session Start — Announce Active Principles

At the start of the **first response** in any new conversation, read `clear/autonomy.yml` and `clear/principles.md`, then output this status block before answering anything:

```
🔒 CLEAR framework active

C · Constrained — verify-ci.sh enforced
L · Limited — autonomy.yml: [found ✅ | NOT FOUND ⚠]
E · Ephemeral — generated files protected
A · Assertive — constraint tests required
R · Reality-Aligned — sources_of_truth: [N concepts | none declared]

humans-only zones: [list paths, or "none"]
verify-ci.sh: [found ✅ | NOT FOUND ⚠]
```

Fill in the bracketed values from the actual files. Show this block **once per conversation**, not on every message. If `clear/autonomy.yml` is missing, tell the user to run the CLEAR installer.

## Core Rule: Never Mark Work Incomplete

After any file edit (create/update/delete), run `./clear/verify-ci.sh`.

- If it fails → fix the issues → run again → repeat until exit code 0
- Never skip or bypass this script
- Before sending a final response for an implementation task, include: verify-ci.sh result (PASS/FAIL), command used, and whether `--fast` or `--fix` was used (if applicable)
- If running in read-only mode (Ask mode), explicitly state: "verify-ci.sh not run because session is read-only."
- Rules guide behavior; CI enforces it. `./clear/verify-ci.sh` is the source of truth

## Autonomy Boundaries [L]

Before modifying any file, check `clear/autonomy.yml`:

| Level | Action |
|-------|--------|
| `full-autonomy` | Proceed freely |
| `supervised` | Generate code, then add "⚠ Human review needed" reminder |
| `humans-only` | **STOP.** Tell the user: "This path is marked humans-only in clear/autonomy.yml. I will not generate code here." |

## Enforced Rules [C]

Your rules are enforced by `clear/verify-ci.sh` (CLEAR-owned) and `clear/verify-local.sh` (project-specific checks). The AI treats verify-ci.sh failures as blockers, not warnings. Common checks it runs:

- Build (TypeScript compilation, no warnings)
- Lint (ESLint, Prettier, Ruff, etc.)  
- Unit and integration tests
- Architecture tests (`tests/architecture/` or `npm run test:architecture`)
- Autonomy boundary validation

## Testing Standards [A]

Write **constraint tests**, not confirmation tests:

```ts
// ❌ Weak — only confirms the current implementation
it('creates a user', () => {
  expect(createUser('a@b.com').email).toBe('a@b.com');
});

// ✅ Strong — enforces an invariant
it('never creates duplicate users for the same email', () => {
  createUser('a@b.com');
  expect(() => createUser('a@b.com')).toThrow();
});
```

- Prefer property-based tests for critical invariants
- Add schema-lock tests for important contracts
- Tests that pass after implementation regeneration are good — validate, don't assume

## Generated Code [E]

- Never hand-edit files with a `// @generated` or `# Generated by` header
- When a source of truth changes (protobuf, OpenAPI, Pydantic model), regenerate derived files using the skill defined for that file type
- See `clear/templates/skills/` for regeneration skill templates

## Source of Truth [R]

Before generating code for any domain concept, check `clear/autonomy.yml`'s `sources_of_truth` section. Derive from that source. When systems conflict, the declared source of truth wins.

## Workflow Template

```
1. User requests: "Implement X"
2. Read clear/autonomy.yml — check affected paths
3. Read clear/principles.md — identify applicable constraints
4. Check sources_of_truth for relevant domain concepts
5. Generate code following all constraints
6. Run ./clear/verify-ci.sh
7. If fails: fix → rerun → repeat until passing
8. Report verify-ci status (PASS/FAIL), command used, and flags used (`--fast`/`--fix` if any)
9. Summarize files changed and remind user to commit
```

## PLAN Mode

For complex changes (new feature, refactor, new domain concept), use PLAN mode first:

```
"Before implementing, show me the plan:
 - What files will be created/modified?
 - What autonomy levels apply?
 - What tests will enforce the new behavior?
 - What source of truth will this derive from?
 Only proceed after I approve the plan."
```

## Repository Layout

```
clear/verify-ci.sh            — Run this. Always. (CLEAR-owned, auto-updated)
clear/verify-local.sh         — Project-specific checks (yours to edit)
clear/autonomy.yml            — Autonomy boundaries + sources of truth
clear/principles.md           — This framework's quick reference
clear/templates/              — Generic templates (architecture tests, skills, linting, CI)
clear/examples/               — Domain-specific examples (architecture tests, skills)
clear/docs/                   — CLEAR documentation
```
